_comment: iTerm2 keybinding profile for /project:launch — Option 6 (Hybrid letters + FZF picker). Implements CPT-73 Option 6.
_instructions: 1. Open iTerm2 → Preferences → Keys → Key Bindings
4. Action: Send Text with vim Special Chars
5. Paste the 'send_text' value below (literal \n at the end includes Enter)
6. Save. Press Cmd+Shift+P in any iTerm2 tab to trigger the picker.

bindings.name: project-picker-fzf
bindings.action: Send Text with vim Special Chars
bindings.rationale: Opens an FZF role picker that reads the current tmux session's windows and jumps to the selected role. Works in any terminal that's attached to a tmux session.

bindings.name: project-picker-fzf-explicit-session
bindings.action: Send Text with vim Special Chars
bindings.rationale: Variant: explicit session name for the choc-skills fleet — useful when running multiple tmux sessions on one machine.

tmux_conf_root_table._comment: Optional tmux root-table binding for zero-prefix navigation. Append to ~/.tmux.conf and run `tmux source-file ~/.tmux.conf`. Implements CPT-73 Option 6 'root-bindings opt-in'.
snippet: # CPT-73 Option 6 — root-table letter bindings (no prefix required).
# Press Ctrl-] then a/b/.../k to jump directly to the corresponding window.
# Letter → role map mirrors CPT-72 ROLE_LETTER.

browser_tab_access_option_7._comment: Option 7 is independent of iTerm2 keybindings — it serves the tmux session via ttyd at http://localhost:7681..7691, one URL per role. Open them as browser tabs for Cmd+1..Cmd+9 navigation.
